title: Nile Red Staining of Drosophila Larval Tissues
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9048g","text":"This protocol is used to stain late larval Drosophila lipid droplets in fat bodies and intestines with Nile Red, which emits fluorescence in the 552/636 nm range. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":43,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"2rq6o","text":"In brief, dissect animals in ice cold phosphate-buffered saline (PBS). Keep tissues in PBS on ice while obtaining your desired sample size. Fix tissues in 4% PFA (diluted in PBS with 0.1% Triton X-100), for 20 minutes, wash 3x in PBS, and stain tissues light-protected at room temperature for 1 hour. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dj7p1","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8nk22","text":"* Do not use any serums for this protocol because Nile Red will instead be drawn away from your tissues and into serum. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"bangh","text":"1. Prepare Nile Red (Sigma-Aldrich) in acetone (1000 ug/mL).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"21lm1","text":"2. Dissect tissues in ice cold PBS. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"asbs4","text":"3. Fix tissues in 4% paraformaldehyde (formalin) diluted in PBS with 0.1% Triton X-100. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"884cj","text":"4. Wash tissues 3x in PBS. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"chack","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fm0ud","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Dissect tissues in ice cold PBS, keeping samples on ice until required sample size is obtained. 1X PBS (Phosphate-buffered saline )
Fix tissues in 4% PFA for 20 minutes. 00:20:00
Wash tissues 3x in PBS. 1X PBS (Phosphate-buffered saline )
Stain samples in Nile Red at 0.5 ug/mL diluted in PBS for 1 hour. 01:00:00 Nile Red prepared in acetone by Elizabeth Allen
Wash tissues 3x in PBS. 1X PBS (Phosphate-buffered saline )

Carefully replace the PBS with mounting medium before transferring samples to slides and imaging. * Image immediately, or temporarily store slides at 4°C. Mounting Media for Immunohistochemistry - Drosophila by Sonia M Hall
creator: Jiajun Li
